articles.title: Cameron Norrie stays positive for Paris despite dismal loss to Thiago Agustín Tirante
articles.description: British No 1 beaten in straight sets at Italian Open ‘I can’t be in better mental and physical shape’ Cameron Norrie believes he is still well positioned for a strong performance at the French Open despite being shocked by his standard of play during Saturday’s frustrating 6-3, 7-5 loss to Thiago Agustín Tirante in the second round of the Italian Open on Friday. Norrie, the British No 1, started the match with a slew of unforced errors, immediately falling 3-0 behind. Those tense early struggles set the tone for a difficult day against one of the most explosive players on the tour. Tirante’s massive first serve consistently scaled 140mph, peaking at 148mph, which the world No 69 backed up by dominating the baseline with his heavy forehand. Continue reading...

articles.title: Scientists say this simple music trick can boost workout endurance by 20%
articles.description: A new study shows that listening to your own favorite workout music can dramatically boost endurance. Cyclists exercising with self-selected songs lasted nearly 20% longer than when riding in silence, yet they didn’t feel more exhausted at the end. Researchers say music may help people stay in the “pain zone” longer without increasing perceived strain.

articles.title: Trump says unaware of a plan to fire FDA commissioner
articles.description: President Donald Trump denies reports he approved firing FDA Commissioner Marty Makary amid agency turmoil and departures. Read more here.

articles.title: CDC Set to Meet Americans From the Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship
articles.description: The cruise ship dealing with a hantavirus outbreak will arrive near the Canary Islands on Sunday morning. The World Health Organization has confirmed a sixth case of the virus. The CDC says the risk to the public remains “extremely low” and has deployed a team to the Canary Islands to meet the 17 Americans trapped on the luxury cruise ship. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdijian reports and Dr. Akshay Syal joins Saturday TODAY to break down what to know about the disease.

articles.description: You know that thing where you’re lying down, still somewhat awake, and suddenly you’re having what can only be described as a strange fever dream? Apparently, that’s not a glitch. According to researchers, your brain was just doing its thing. articles.title: Iran war sparks swap line requests
articles.description: In April, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent noted requests for permanent swap lines from the United Arab Emirates and other economies weathering the Iran War. Swap lines, typically deployed in emergencies, enable currency exchanges between central banks to prevent global dollar shortages. Recent comments from Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, signal his willingness to collaborate with the Treasury on international issues. CNBC’s Matt Peterson reports.
